data.title: Financials Down on Weak Jobs Data — Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions slid as weak jobs data and a new batch of punitive tariffs weighed on sentiment.

The CBOE volatility index, known as the "fear gauge" because it measures the cost of insuring stock portfolios, shot up by more than 20%, as traders braced for stagflation, following elevated inflation and weak jobs data.

In response to the jobs data, President Donald Trump said that he ordered his team to fire Bureau of Labor Statistics Commissioner Erika McEntarfer.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

Site Centers Corp expected to post a loss of 13 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Site Centers Corp , is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Beachwood Ohio-based company is expected to report a 75.6% decrease in revenue to $27.807 million from $114.13 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 3 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Site Centers Corp is for a loss of 13 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Site Centers Corp is $14.75, about 27.9% above its last closing price of $10.63

data.title: Industrials Down After Lackluster Jobs Growth, Weak Factory Data — Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies declined after lackluster jobs data and a slew of new tariff developments.

In one silver lining for cyclical companies, the weak jobs data could trigger a Federal Reserve rate cut in September.

"We still believe the macro environment is favorable for small [capitalization stocks], with Fed cuts on the horizon, high-yield spreads below 3%, and balance sheets in very good shape," said strategists at brokerage Jefferies, in a note to clients.

The Institute for Supply Management said its purchasing managers' index of manufacturing activity contracted to 48 in July from 49 in June, underscoring the slowing economic activity portrayed in the jobs report.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.description: (21:39 GMT) Independence Realty Trust Price Target Raised to $24.00/Share From $22.00 by Compass Point

data.title: Mistral in talks with VC firms, MGX to raise funds at $10 billion valuation, FT reports
data.description:
French artificial intelligence startup Mistral is in talks with investors, venture capital firms and Abu Dhabi's MGX to raise $1 billion at a valuation of $10 billion, the Financial Times reported on Friday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The company launched in June Europe's first AI reasoning model, which uses logical thinking to create a response, as it tries to keep pace with American and Chinese rivals at the forefront of AI development.

The funding would accelerate the commercial rollout of Mistral's Le Chat chatbot and support continued development of its large language models, the report said.

MGX and Mistral did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

The startup raised 600 million euros in a Series B funding round that valued the company at 5.8 billion euros last year.

Industry observers consider Mistral as Europe's best-positioned AI company to rival Silicon Valley leaders, though the French firm has yet to achieve comparable market traction or revenue scale.

Mistral counts Nvidia , Andreessen Horowitz and Lightspeed Venture Partners among its investors.

data.title: Sadot Group Names Sansom CFO
data.description:
By Natalie Weger

Sadot Group named Paul Sansom chief financial officer of the global food supply chain company, effective Aug. 1.

Sansom succeeds Jennifer Black, who resigned as CFO on July 28. Black has agreed to serve as a consultant to the company for 60 days.

He was previously a member of the company's board and its audit committee. In line with his appointment as CFO, Sansom has resigned from those positions. He also serves as a board member and group chief financial officer of InterGen UK Limited, Sadot said.

Sansom will receive an annual base salary of $190,000 for the first six months of employment. At the end of that period, Sansom will be subject to a base salary increase to a minimum of $380,000 a year, according to the company.

Write to Natalie Weger at natalie.weger@wsj.com

data.title: STARTENGINE CROWDFUNDING, INC. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc., a leading platform for equity crowdfunding and private offerings, has released its latest Form 10-Q report, showcasing significant financial and operational improvements. The report highlights the company's robust growth in revenue and profitability, driven by the success of its new product lines and strategic operational adjustments.

Financial Highlights

StartEngine Crowdfunding, Inc. reported impressive financial results for the period, reflecting substantial growth and improved profitability:

  • Total Revenues: $70.18 million, a remarkable increase of 225% from the same period in 2024, primarily driven by the success of StartEngine Private offerings.
  • Gross Profit: $22.31 million, an increase of $11.63 million compared to the same period in 2024, indicating improved profitability.
  • Operating Income: $4.96 million, a substantial improvement from an operating loss of $6.98 million in the same period in 2024, highlighting effective cost management and increased revenue.
  • Net Income: $4.99 million, a turnaround from a net loss of $7.04 million in the same period in 2024, demonstrating strong financial performance.
  • Diluted EPS: $0.00, reflecting the company's improved earnings position compared to a loss per share in the same period in 2024.

Business Highlights

The company's business performance has been bolstered by strategic initiatives and new product launches:

  • Revenue Segments: The company reported significant growth in its StartEngine Private product line, which became the largest revenue generator in 2025. This product line involves providing accredited investors the opportunity to purchase membership interests in funds that own shares of venture capital-backed, late-stage private companies.
  • Revenue Segments: Regulation Crowdfunding platform fees saw a slight increase due to higher raise totals for issuers within the period, while Regulation A commissions increased significantly due to higher amounts raised in offerings.
  • Revenue Segments: StartEngine Premium revenue increased due to a shift in payment strategy, more issuers utilizing the services, and a price increase from $15,000 to $17,000.
  • Revenue Segments: StartEngine Secure revenue increased due to a change in billing strategy to a tiered service annual contract.
  • New Product Launches: The company launched 'StartEngine Private' in August 2023, which has quickly become the largest source of revenue, indicating a successful new product introduction.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on the StartEngine Private product line as a major revenue source, although growth may be limited by factors such as stock availability and demand.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates an increase in Regulation A and Crowdfunding revenues as raises continue to disburse, and it plans to complete the transition of hosting new Regulation Crowdfunding issuances with its broker-dealer entity by the end of 2025.
  • Operational Adjustments: At the end of 2024, the company reduced its workforce by 25% to remain competitive and reduce costs, aiming for profitability in 2025.
  • Operational Adjustments: The company is leveraging more technology to improve process efficiency and scale the business, as evidenced by an increase in software expenses.

SEC Filing: STARTENGINE CROWDFUNDING, INC. [ STGC ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: Methanex Is Maintained at Outperformer by CIBC
Materials Down After Weak Jobs Data, Copper Rout — Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials fell after weak jobs data and tariff moves that caused a plunge for copper prices.

Copper futures tumbled 23%, $4.41 per pound, this week for their largest weekly decline on record, going back to 1968.

The tariff levied on copper imports was not as broad as feared.

On the eve of the Trump administration's tariff deadline, stores of the industrial metal in the U.S. were priced at a 30% premium to London copper, as arbitrageurs moved metal across the Atlantic to play the spread in prices.

Trump cited Canada's retaliation to his April warnings when he signed an executive order raising tariffs to 35% from 25% on roughly 15% of Canadian imports that aren't compliant with the USMCA trade agreement.

Gold futures ticked up as traders sought out safe havens.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Amerant Bancorp Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Amerant Bancorp Inc., a prominent financial institution primarily operating in South Florida, the greater Tampa area, Houston, and New York City, has released its latest Form 10-Q report. The report highlights the company's robust financial performance and strategic operational shifts aimed at enhancing its market position and efficiency.

Financial Highlights

Amerant Bancorp Inc. has demonstrated strong financial performance in its latest quarter, as evidenced by the following key metrics:

  • Total Interest Income: $297.5 million, reflecting a slight increase from the previous year due to higher interest from investment securities.
  • Net Interest Income: $176.4 million, showing an improvement driven by a reduction in interest expense.
  • Net Income: $34.96 million, a significant increase compared to the previous year, attributed to lower provision for credit losses and higher noninterest income.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $0.83, indicating a strong performance compared to the same period last year.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $0.83, consistent with basic earnings per share, reflecting no dilution effect.

Business Highlights

In addition to its financial achievements, Amerant Bancorp Inc. has made significant strides in its business operations:

  • Geographical Performance: The company remains heavily dependent on the economic conditions in South Florida, the greater Tampa area, Houston, and the five New York City boroughs. As of June 30, 2025, commercial real estate loans held for investment in Florida, Houston, New York, and other regions were $2.1 billion, $158 million, $219 million, and $224 million, respectively.
  • Operational Focus: In April 2025, Amerant Bancorp announced a strategic shift to focus its mortgage business on in-footprint mortgage lending to support its retail and private banking customer base in Florida, transitioning away from being a national mortgage originator.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to progressively reduce its full-time equivalent (FTE) count from 77 to approximately 20 by the early part of the fourth quarter of 2025. This includes transferring loans into the core platform and exiting or modifying existing vendor contracts.
  • Segment Information: Amerant Bancorp is managed as a single operating segment, with the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) as the Chief Operating Decision Maker (CODM). The CEO reviews net income as the measure of profit or loss and assesses the performance of the consolidated entity.
  • Loan Portfolio Management: The company utilizes an internal risk rating system to monitor the credit quality of its loan portfolio, which is updated continuously. Loans are grouped into five master risk categories to monitor potential loss risks and activate collection or recovery processes as needed.
  • Stock Repurchase Program: The company has an ongoing stock repurchase program, with the Board of Directors approving an increase in the amount available for repurchases to $25 million as of May 28, 2025. In the first half of 2025, the company repurchased 491,093 shares of Class A common stock.
  • Houston Sale Transaction: During the second quarter of 2024, Amerant Bancorp entered into a Purchase and Assumption Agreement with MidFirst Bank to sell certain assets and liabilities of its Houston banking operations, including six branches. This transaction involved non-routine expenses totaling approximately $5.5 million.

SEC Filing: Amerant Bancorp Inc. [ AMTB ] - 10-Q - Aug. 01, 2025

data.title: DMC Global Inc expected to post earnings of 6 cents a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• DMC Global Inc is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on August 5 for the period ending June 30 2025

  • The Broomfield Colorado-based company is expected to report a 11.4% decrease in revenue to $151.6 million from $171.18 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 3 analysts, based on LSEG data.The company's guidance on May 1 2025, for the period ended June 30, was for revenue between $149.00 million and $157.00 million.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for DMC Global Inc is for earnings of 6 cents per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 1 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for DMC Global Inc is $10.25, about 21.1% above its last closing price of $8.09

The company's guidance on May 1 2025 for the period ended June 30 was for Earnings before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation and Amortization of between USD149 million and USD13 million.

Previous quarterly performance (using preferred earnings measure in US dollars). ​

data.title: Consumer Cos Down on Tepid Jobs Data — Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer discretionary stocks fell after tepid jobs data.

Consumer spending has proved resilient despite more than three years of elevated inflation.

Some retailers and consumer-products companies, such as Procter & Gamble, warned in their latest updates that shoppers are exhibiting more caution.

A rising unemployment rate could be the last straw for the consumer-led economic expansion.

Shares of Rolex producer Watches of Switzerland fell by 7% after President Trump imposed tariffs of 39% on Swiss imports.

Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

data.title: Microvast's CFO Leaves After Three Months
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Microvast Chief Financial Officer Carl Schultz has departed roughly three months after starting in the role.

Schultz ceased to be employed on July 29, the battery maker said Friday, without giving additional details.

He started in the role in late April, succeeding Fariyal Khanbabi. He was making a base salary of $420,000, according to a separate filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

He previously served as chief financial officer of Air Liquide's North American business starting in 2022.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Ethereum Lost 5.21% to $3540.92 — Data Talk
data.description:
Ethereum is down $194.55 today or 5.21% to $3540.92

  • Lowest 5 p.m. level since July 18, 2025 when it traded at $3521.19
  • Largest percentage decrease since June 22, 2025 when it dropped 9.05%
  • Down four of the past five days
  • Down two consecutive days, down 6.08% over this period
  • Worst two day stretch since the two days ending June 22, 2025 when it fell 9.95%
  • Down 3.94% this week
  • Worst weekly performance since the week ending June 20, 2025 when it fell 4.71%
  • Up 5.87% year-to-date
  • Down 27.23% from its all-time intraday high of $4865.81 on Nov. 10, 2021
  • Up 17.41% from 52 weeks ago (Aug. 2, 2024), when it traded at $3015.95
  • Down 13.76% from its 52-week intraday high of $4105.92 on Dec. 16, 2024
  • Up 151.60% from its 52-week intraday low of $1387.85 on April 9, 2025
  • Traded as low as $3491.86; lowest intraday level since July 18, 2025 when it hit $3421.32
  • Down 6.52% at today's intraday low; Largest intraday % decrease since June 22, 2025 when it was down as much as 11.81%

Note: The Ethereum price is a 5 p.m. ET snapshot from Kraken

Data compiled by Dow Jones Market Data

data.title: Magnitude 5 earthquake strikes Hindu Kush region in Afghanistan, EMSC says
data.description:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck the Hindu Kush region in Afghanistan on Saturday, the European Mediterranean Seismological Centre said.

The quake was at a depth of 123 kilometers (76.43 miles), EMSC said.

EMSC initially reported the magnitude as 5.5.
